REPLY TO PLTF'S RESPONSES TO DEFT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES FILED BY DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING TO INCLUDE THE RULING ON THE DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS: SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION GIVEN THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AT PARAGRAPH 12, HER JUNE 7, 2006 EMAIL TO HENSON AND HER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY TO THE EFFECT THAT THE EYEWASH STATIONS WERE NONCOMPLIANT. PLAINTIFF HAS STATED A PRIMA FACIE CASE FOR RETALIATION AND THE CITY HAS PROFFERED A BONA FIDE REASON FOR TERMINATION. IN TURN, PLAINTIFF'S STATEMENT TO FINLEY OF PLAINTIFF'S INTENT TO REPORT VIOLATIONS TO OHSA, FOLLOWED BY FINLEY PLACING PLAINTIFF ON A DEVELOPMENT PLAN, PLAINTIFF'S ACTUAL COMPLAINT TO OHSA AND SHORTLY THEREAFTER PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION, PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT WOULD SUPPORT A FINDING OF PRETEXT. SEE GREEN V. RALEE ENGINEERING CO. (1998) 19 CAL.4TH 66. COURT ISSUES THE FOLLOWING RULINGS ON THE CITY'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS: OBJECTIONS 6, 19, 20 AND 23 ARE SUSTAINED AND THE REMAINDER OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. AFTER THE SESSION CONCLUDES, COURT SIGNS AN ORDER ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. AN ORDER IS SIGNED ON THE MOTION DURING THE REGULAR COURT PROCEEDING. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: STEPHEN F. HENRY, ESQUIRE (510.898.1883) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JONATHAN C. ROLNICK, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3815) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING TO INCLUDE THE RULING ON THE DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS: Summary adjudication is granted as to the 1st cause of action given that plaintiff does not oppose the motion.
The motion is denied as to the 2nd cause of action based on plaintiff's declaration at paragraph 12, her June 7, 2006 email to Henson and her deposition testimony to the effect that the eyewash stations were noncompliant. Plaintiff has stated a prima facie case for retaliation and the City has proffered a bona fide reason for termination. In turn, plaintiff's statement to Finley of plaintiff's intent to report violations to OHSA, followed by Finley placing plaintiff on a development plan, plaintiff's actual complaint to OHSA and shortly thereafter plaintiff's termination, presents evidence that would support a finding of pretext. See Green v. Ralee Engineering Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 66. COURT ISSUES THE FOLLOWING RULINGS ON THE CITY'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS: OBJECTIONS 6, 19, 20 AND 23 ARE SUSTAINED AND THE REMAINDER OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. AFTER THE SESSION CONCLUDES, COURT SIGNS AN ORDER ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. AN ORDER IS SIGNED ON THE MOTION DURING THE REGULAR COURT PROCEEDING. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
